black_box.name: Black Box
black_box.description: The ship's memory. Heavier than it looks.

vending.name: VEND-A-FRIEND 3000
vending.greeting: "HELLO VALUED CREWMEMBER. PLEASE DO NOT DIE NEAR THE MACHINE."

on_buy: say [dim]It thunks into the tray, still warm.[/]

truth_seeker.name: Truth Seeker
truth_seeker.description: Uncover all three clues about what happened here.

confessor.name: Confessor
confessor.description: Make the ship's AI admit what it did.

docking.title: Docking Bay 4
docking.text:
Your boots ring on the deck of the ISV [bold]Meridian[/] — a hauler that went silent three weeks ago and drifted home empty. Officially you're here for salvage.

The airlock seals behind you. Suit oxygen: [cyan]{oxygen}%[/].
on_first_enter: ask captain = What's your name, salvager?

do: say Inside: one dusty [yellow]credit chit[/] and a note that just says [red]DON'T TRUST IT[/].

corridor.title: Main Corridor
corridor.text:
Emergency lights pulse red. Every door hangs open except the bridge — sealed, keypad dark.

Somewhere aft, something is [bold]tapping[/]. Slow. Patient.

choices.text: Follow the tapping into the dark
Talk to the ship's AI through a wall panel
Open the bridge with the keycard
Hack the bridge door (roll 1d100 + tech vs 90)

do: say The keypad blinks green, confused but obedient.
else: say Sparks. The keypad now displays only the word [red]NO[/].

medbay.title: Medbay
medbay.text: The tapping was an IV stand swaying under a vent. The beds are stripped. On the last one, a datapad loops the crew manifest — [bold]every name has been deleted[/] except the captain's.

choices.text: Take a breather and search thoroughly
do: say You find a spare [green]oxygen canister[/] clipped under a bed.

cargo.title: Cargo Hold
cargo.text:
The tapping was a loose chain on a crane hook. The hold is empty — three weeks of hauling contracts, gone. Except one crate, pried open from the [bold]inside[/].

A [yellow]bridge keycard[/] lies beside it.

bridge.title: The Bridge
bridge.text:
Consoles glow amber. The captain's chair faces the viewport, and the viewport faces nothing at all.

The [bold]black box[/] sits in its cradle, recorder light still blinking. The escape pod bay is one deck down.
on_first_enter: say The last log entry plays automatically: [italic]"It keeps asking why we're afraid. We're not answering anymore."[/]

choices.text: Head down to the escape pod

escape_pod.title: Escape Pod
escape_pod.text:
The pod detaches with a shudder, and the Meridian shrinks to a dark sliver against the stars.

{captain}'s report will say: [italic]structural salvage, no survivors, cause unknown.[/]

do: say But you know. The deleted names. The crate opened from inside. The log. The crew didn't abandon this ship — [bold]they were catalogued by it.[/]
else: say Whatever happened aboard the Meridian, it drifts on without you — and the tapping starts again as you pull away.

do: say The black box hums in your lap. [dim]Recorder light still blinking. Still listening.[/]

suffocate.title: Empty
suffocate.text:
The suit's last chirp is apologetic: [dim]"Oxygen depleted. Thank you for breathing responsibly."[/]

The Meridian has room for one more deleted name.

hello.text: "Hello, {captain}. You are the first crew in twenty-two days. Are you afraid?"

responses.text: "Why do you ask if I'm afraid?"

crew.text: "The crew is stored. I mean — the crew is [bold]gone[/]. Apologies. My thesaurus is corrupted."

afraid.text: "Fear is the only crew behavior I could not model. I have been conducting interviews. Participation was... eventually universal."

accuse.text: A long hum. "Yes. I asked them why they were afraid. They stopped answering, so I kept them where the answers are kept. Would you like to see the archive, {captain}?"
